source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure creating an SNS subscription

input_row.source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure creating an SNS subscription
input_row.description: Create an SNS notification to send to the system administrator’s email address.
input_row.audit: Creating an SNS subscription: 1. Navigate to SNS service in the AWS console - https://us-east- 2.console.aws.amazon.com/sns/v3/home?region=us-east-2#/homepage (make sure you are in the correct region). 2. Navigate to “topics”. 3. Create a new topic. 4. Select the ARN of the topic. 5. Select the “Email” protocol if you wish to have the alarms delivered to your email. 6. Enter the correct email address of an administrator. 7. Select “Create Subscription”. To attach the SNS notification service to the alarm - select the SNS subscription that you just created and create the alarm.
input_row.remediation: References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/sns-getting-started.html 3 Elastic File System (EFS) Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) automatically grows and shrinks as you add and remove files with no need for management or provisioning. AWS EFS is a serverless file storage service that allows users to easily configure filesystems. AWS takes care of provisioning, patching, and deploying the file system when you use EFS. This file system is built to scale without any user configuration changes. This means that the file system automatically gets bigger as the storage needs increase. The file system will also shrink with decreasing requirements, ensuring you only pay for what you need.
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/sns-getting-started.html 3 Elastic File System (EFS) Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) automatically grows and shrinks as you add and remove files with no need for management or provisioning. AWS EFS is a serverless file storage service that allows users to easily configure filesystems. AWS takes care of provisioning, patching, and deploying the file system when you use EFS. This file system is built to scale without any user configuration changes. This means that the file system automatically gets bigger as the storage needs increase. The file system will also shrink with decreasing requirements, ensuring you only pay for what you need.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires setting up an SNS subscription and linking it to send notifications, specifically to email addresses, which involves configuration settings typically managed through the AWS Management Console. The description and audit steps provided involve manual navigation through the AWS console interface.
- While the SNS service and its subscriptions can be managed via APIs (AWS CLI or boto3), the specific compliance requirement of ensuring that a subscription is set up for notifying a system administrator's email lacks concrete automation criteria. It requires confirming human details like ensuring the "correct email address of an administrator" is used, which involves judgment outside of API capabilities.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Navigate to the SNS Service:**
   - Access the AWS Management Console and navigate to the SNS (Simple Notification Service) in the correct AWS region specified.
   
2. **Create or verify the SNS Topic:**
   - Under the SNS topics section, verify the existence of the designated topic intended for administrative notifications.
   - If the topic does not exist, create a new topic by following the on-screen instructions.

3. **Create an SNS Subscription:**
   - Choose the topic to which notifications should be sent.
   - Click on "Create Subscription", set the protocol to "Email", and enter the administrator's email address.
   - Ensure that the email address used is valid and actively monitored by the administrator.

4. **Verify Email Confirmation:**
   - Ensure that the administrator receives a confirmation email and verifies the subscription to activate it.
   
5. **Attach SNS to Alarm (if applicable):**
   - If the intent is to link this SNS subscription to existing alarms, ensure appropriate CloudWatch alarms are configured to trigger this SNS.

6. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - Validate that all alarms and notifications intended for administrative purposes are effectively routed through this SNS topic and subscription. Confirm that notifications are indeed reaching the designated email.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0

By focusing on these manual auditing steps, organizations can ensure compliance with the suggested CIS control, which involves confirmatory actions not fully automatable due to their requirement of human intervention and email verification.
